User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user in the ATLAS application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

archetype.description: The user's chosen archetype (e.g., Titan, Sage, Maverick).

userName.description: The user's display name or username.

bodyType.description: The user's selected starting body type.

fireteamId.description: Reference to the Fireteam this user belongs to. (Relationship: Fireteam 1:N User)

guildId.description: Reference to the Guild this user belongs to.

lastLogTimestamp.description: The Unix timestamp of the user's last logged activity.

userSkills.description: A map of skills the user has interacted with, tracking unlock status and XP. Keys are skill IDs.

avatarLayers.description: A map of visual layers active on the user's avatar. Keys are layer names, values are true.

momentumFlameActive.description: Whether the user's daily activity streak (Momentum Flame) is active.

gems.description: The user's balance of premium currency.

streakFreezes.description: The number of streak-repair items the user owns.

traits.description: A map of traits the user has earned. Keys are trait IDs, values are true.

verificationVotes.description: The total number of verification votes this user has cast.

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Represents a skill in the Nebula skill tree.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Skill entity.

description.description: A brief description of the skill.

category.description: The category of the skill (Physical, Mental, Social, Practical, Creative).

pioneerUserId.description: Reference to the User who first discovered/created this skill. (Relationship: User 1:N Skill (Pioneer))

prerequisites.description: An array of skill IDs that must be unlocked before this skill can be acquired.

cost.description: The cost to unlock this skill.

innovatorAwarded.description: A flag to ensure the Innovator trait is only awarded once for this skill.

Log.title: Log
Log.description: Represents a user's activity log entry.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Log entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who performed the activity. (Relationship: User 1:N Log)

skillId.description: Reference to the Skill associated with this log entry. (Relationship: Skill 1:N Log)

timestamp.description: The timestamp of when the activity was performed.

xp.description: The amount of experience points gained from this activity.

verificationPhotoUrl.description: URL of the photo/video used to verify the activity.

isVerified.description: Whether the log entry has been verified by the community.

Fireteam.title: Fireteam
Fireteam.description: Represents a fireteam (a group of up to 5 users).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Fireteam entity.

region.description: The region (city) where the fireteam is based.

state.description: The state where the fireteam is based.

country.description: The country where the fireteam is based.

ownerId.description: The user ID of the fireteam's creator.

members.description: A map of user IDs who are members of this fireteam. Keys are user IDs, values are true.

streakActive.description: Whether the Fireteam's Soul Link is active.

streakStartDate.description: The Unix timestamp when the current active streak began.

Quest.title: Quest
Quest.description: Represents a quest assigned to a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quest entity.

description.description: A description of what the user needs to do to complete the quest.

category.description: The skill category this quest is related to (e.g., Physical, Mental).

isCompleted.description: Whether the user has completed the quest.

userId.description: The ID of the user this quest is assigned to.

Territory.title: Territory
Territory.description: Represents a Faction Challenge that Fireteams can compete in.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Territory Challenge.

faction.description: The skill category this challenge is associated with.

challengeDescription.description: A description of the challenge (e.g., 'Log the most running distance this week').

endsAt.description: The Unix timestamp when the challenge ends.

scores.description: A map of Fireteam IDs to their scores in this challenge.

awarded.description: A flag to track if the 'State Best' trait has been awarded for this challenge cycle.

Trait.title: Trait
Trait.description: Represents a personality trait or achievement a user can earn.

name.description: The display name of the trait (e.g., 'Pioneer').

description.description: A brief description of what the trait represents.

icon.description: The key for the lucide-react icon associated with this trait.

Guild.title: Guild
Guild.description: Represents a large, user-run community focused on a specific skill category.

category.description: The skill category this Guild is focused on.

region.description: The region (city) where the Guild is based.

ownerId.description: The user ID of the Guild's creator.

members.description: A map of user IDs who are members of this Guild. Keys are user IDs, values are true.

definition.description: Stores user profile data. Path-based ownership enables simple security rules.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Stores skill data created by pioneer user.

params.name: skillId
params.description: The unique identifier for the skill.

definition.description: Stores logs, for the activities realized by each user and the skills they are trained on. Enables filtering and aggregation of logs based on user and skill.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique identifier for the log.

definition.description: Stores fireteam data. The 'members' map is denormalized for authorization independence, removing the need for `get()` calls in security rules.

params.name: fireteamId
params.description: The unique identifier for the fireteam.

definition.description: Stores personalized quests for each user. Enables private quest tracking and management.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: questId
params.description: The unique identifier for the quest.

definition.description: Stores data about Faction Challenges that Fireteams can compete in.

params.name: territoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the territory challenge.

definition.description: Stores global definitions for all available traits.

params.name: traitId
params.description: The unique identifier for the trait.

definition.description: Stores data for large, user-run communities (Guilds).

params.name: guildId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Guild.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support ATLAS's core features with a focus on authorization independence, scalability, and clear data modeling. User data is stored under `/users/{userId}`, enabling simple ownership-based security rules. Skills are stored under `/skills/{skillId}`, created by pioneer users and associated to skills via the Logs. Logs are created under `/users/{userId}/logs/{logId}`, enabling tracking the skills used by users and the XP earned. Fireteams are stored under `/fireteams/{fireteamId}` with denormalized membership data to enforce team-based logic (Soul Link) without complex `get()` calls. Territories are now Faction Challenges, stored in a top-level collection for public access and competition. Traits are stored globally in `/traits/{traitId}` as they are definitions, while user-specific earned traits are stored within the `/users/{userId}` document itself. Guilds are stored in a new top-level collection `/guilds/{guildId}` to facilitate public discovery and regional filtering. This structure supports required QAPs, such as listing skills by user (using a collection group query with the `userId` field in Logs), and listing Fireteams by region (using a composite index on `region` and `state`).

Authorization Independence is achieved by storing the `userId` directly within the Log documents. The Fireteam `members` map is denormalized so all access control decisions are made locally to the document without requiring server-side `get()` operations.

Structural Segregation is applied to private user data (profile, logs) versus shared team data. The Fireteam data has a homogenous security posture in that all members have the same access (read access and write if they are the owner), and is segregated from user data.
